title: Emotional Trauma
wikitext:
[]: Emotional Trauma             Quote Post by Evangelion Oi! » Tue Oct 17, 2006 10:32 pm i think Asuka had the most emotional trma in the seris though Misato was close i think Shinji was just a whiner Rei is the one with the less truma i NEED to learn to spell... :oops:

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Oct 17, 2006 10:51 pm what?

[]:             Quote Post by Nukie » Tue Oct 17, 2006 10:55 pm Lets not "compare traumas." I think we can all agree all our main players went through some pretty bad shit. These traumas affected them in a profound way. Comparing them just seems dumb.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 17, 2006 10:56 pm Spelling, puctuation, grammer, and proper sentence structure are your friends. :P *Moving this to Discussion.* Personally, I would have to say that Asuka was the most traumatized of any of the characters. Many of the characters have irrational degrees of baggage in association with their trauma, but when it comes to Asuka, the fact that she managed to survive for so long without unraveling is remarkable. If it weren't for her mental rape at the hands of Arael, she would have continued on for some time. She may never have truly dealt with her problems, but she is remarkably functional despite her deeply troubled past. I mean, being the one to discover your parent hanging from the ceiling at a tender young age is something that could traumatize anyone at any age, let alone someone so early on in development.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Oct 17, 2006 11:07 pm Even more so considering that before she died, Asuka's mom (what was her name?), more or less made it absolutely clear to Asuka that she wasn't loved or needed anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Tue Oct 17, 2006 11:17 pm Well, at least she died knowing her mother loved her.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Tue Oct 17, 2006 11:31 pm Asuka was actually the strongest character when it comes down to dealing with her trauma, like Monkey said, she was at least somewhat functional, unlike Shinji but just wallowed in self hatred, but Arael fucked it all up. At least she got to know her mother had actually been with her all time before getting her ass kicked by the Harpies.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Oct 18, 2006 2:06 am All other woes pale in comparision to Shinji's.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Wed Oct 18, 2006 4:13 am In my opinion, Shinji was the part of Anno that overreacts and sees more trouble for himself than there really is -- the hypochondriac (also, the projector of hatred). He places more blame on the others around him for not being loved, when it's really because he doesn't love himself. Also, keep in mind what he has to do in the end... that's fairly traumatic. And he has the most traumatic time in EoE (at least Asuka sort of comes to a revelation before she is... well, you know.) Misato and Shinji have very similar self-doubt problems, but deal with them in completely opposite ways.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 18, 2006 8:22 am zeaven wrote: In my opinion, Shinji was the part of Anno that overreacts and sees more trouble for himself than there really is

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 18, 2006 9:23 am Unless your mom becomes a giant, green-eyed monster and you are abandoned by your daddy as a wee babe, you can't truly identify with Shinji, either. ::p Threads like this are absolutely inane.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Oct 18, 2006 9:40 am I would say that #22' (especially the NPC bath scene where she wallows in self-pity and loathing towards Shinj, Misato, and especially herself) indicates that she was already on the verge of some sort of major emotional meltdown; her traumatic encounter with Arael simply pushed her over the edge.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Oct 18, 2006 9:56 am Shin-seiki wrote: I would say that #22' (especially the NPC bath scene where she wallows in self-pity and loathing towards Shinj, Misato, and especially herself) indicates that she was already on the verge of some sort of major emotional meltdown; her traumatic encounter with Arael simply pushed her over the edge.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 18, 2006 10:17 am slothen wrote: as to reichu, I always thought the idea that Yui was somewhere inside unit 01 protecting him was kinda uplifting.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 18, 2006 1:08 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: You can't do that with Asuka, her depression is her own, and unless your mother committed suicide, you can't identify with her depression.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Wed Oct 18, 2006 2:39 pm Reichu wrote: Unless your mom becomes a giant, green-eyed monster and you are abandoned by your daddy as a wee babe, you can't truly identify with Shinji, either. ::p

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Wed Oct 18, 2006 3:12 pm I think the beauty of Evangelion is you can identify with the characters in any way at all. People can identify with Shinji and Asuka because while their problems are at an extreme end of the spectrum everyone will have feelings of self doubt and the feeling of worthlessness at one point of their lives. It's just Asuka and Shinji are more extreme cases of it. I think out of Asuka and Shinji none of them have it any worse then each other. Although I do feel more sympathy for Asuka because everything seems to go wrong for her so suddenly. I do feel sympathy for Shinji aswel though because he has to witness everything going wrong for everyone else while its going wrong for him and he feels as if he's to blame for it all. I find it a bit unfair to call Shinji a whiner though, because I know if I was in his situation i'd be doing my fair share of whining aswel.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Wed Oct 18, 2006 3:43 pm jcmoorehead wrote: I find it a bit unfair to call Shinji a whiner though, because I know if I was in his situation i'd be doing my fair share of whining aswel.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 18, 2006 5:41 pm Part of the reason I love this series so much is because I really idenitfy in terms of some of the character's emotional problems. Especially because of the depression/anxiety problems Shinji has of thinking he's worthless, while Asuka just wants to be loved/emotional receptivity (ie: "You won't even hold me!" 22') , feeling she doesn't get that from anyone... The anti-hero aspect of espeically Shinji and Misato really appeals to me, too. It just makes the characters all the more human and real. With Misato, she knows she's not perfect, but does what she can to get through life (despite having only surface-level realtionships...) In terms of comparing traumas, techinally all the main players (Shinji, Misato, Asuka, Rei(? Well, Gendou only sees her as a tool/doll, so I guess so)) lost one or both parents and just do what they can to get by; though it does seem like Asuka (from what we see in the series/movies) had it the worst in terms of psycho-Kyouko telling her she's not wanted/loved and should just die. That right there is screwed up, and it's amazing as other people pointed out that she was able to hang on for as long as she did, despite starting to finally lose it in 22. ( Before the 15th's attack, as someone already pointed out!) Asuka, 22' wrote: "I feel disgusting. ( kimochi warui ) Who would want to be in the same bathtub as Misato and stupid Shinji? Who would wash their clothes using the same washing machine as Misato and stupid Shinji? Who would want to sit on the same toilet as Misato and stupid Shinji? Who would want to breath the same air as Misato and stupid Shinji? I hate Misato! I hate Shinji! I hate the First even more! I hate papa! I hate mama! But I hate myself the most!! I can't take it anymore!! Why me?! Me?!"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 18, 2006 8:52 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Reichu wrote: Unless your mom becomes a giant, green-eyed monster and you are abandoned by your daddy as a wee babe, you can't truly identify with Shinji, either. ::p
